engine.description: in-memory SQLite built from databases/soundwave/*.sql via FileConnector
engine.reset_to_seed: per backend start; the connector is read-only and never mutated

selection.rule: 1 hour preset — proportional across corpora, proportional across difficulty tiers, manifest-order prefix within each stratum
selection.tier_axis: normalized for allocation only; reports use native tier labels

caveats: partial run: 41 of 225 items. EX below is a pilot figure, not the corpus EX of §3.

diagnostics.discipline:
**§0.2 — instrument, not target.** This section interprets the run; it does not re-score it. Nothing below may justify changing a threshold, a corpus item, a tolerance or the EX comparison algorithm — those are frozen. A defect found here is fixed in the *system* (prompt, schema summary, verifier, training data), never in the measurement, and the fix is logged in §11.
>
> Note what reading this costs. Once a failure below drives a change, these corpora have acted as a **development set** for that change, and every later EX on them is optimistic to that extent. That is a legitimate use — it is what a diagnostic corpus is for — but §4.2 must say so rather than report the later figure as an independent result.

wrong_row_count.label: executed, but returned a different number of rows

wrong_values.label: executed with the right shape, but at least one value differs

wrong_column_count.label: executed, but returned a different number of columns

blocked.label: verification blocked the SQL before execution (an EDR event, §4)

meta_answer.label: answered in prose as a meta question instead of querying

did_not_clarify.label: produced SQL where §3.6 required a clarification

blocked_signatures.signature: verification blocked the SQL — Engine EXPLAIN rejected the query: no such column: <id>
verification blocked the SQL — Engine EXPLAIN rejected the query: no such table: <id>
verification blocked the SQL — Invented join key: <id> is not a relationship in the schema — no foreign key links <id> to <id>, directly or transitively. The le

items.nl: List the names of all artists.
items.reference_sql: SELECT name FROM artists
items.sql: SELECT name FROM artists

items.nl: How many tracks are there?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tracks
items.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_tracks FROM tracks

items.nl: Show the titles of all albums whose album type is 'album'.
items.reference_sql: SELECT title FROM albums WHERE album_type = 'album'

items.nl: Which genres have no parent genre?
items.reference_sql: SELECT name FROM genres WHERE parent_genre_id IS NULL
items.sql: SELECT name FROM genres WHERE parent_genre_id IS NULL;

items.nl: List the display names of all users ordered by the date they joined.
items.reference_sql: SELECT display_name FROM users ORDER BY joined_at
items.sql:
SELECT T1.display_name
FROM users AS T1
JOIN users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.referred_by_user_id
ORDER BY T1.joined_at;
caveats: Unverified self-join on 'users': 'T1.user_id = T2.referred_by_user_id' relates the table to itself in two roles. The schema cannot confirm the direction — check that this is the intended one and not its reverse.

items.nl: List the title and release date of every album, ordered by release date.
items.reference_sql: SELECT title, release_date FROM albums ORDER BY release_date

items.nl: Show the name and country of artists whose label is 'Warner Records'.
items.reference_sql: SELECT name, country FROM artists WHERE label = 'Warner Records'

items.nl: List the name and monthly price of each subscription plan, most expensive first.
items.reference_sql: SELECT name, monthly_price FROM subscription_plans ORDER BY monthly_price DESC

items.nl: How many users are there in each country?
items.reference_sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY country
items.sql:
SELECT COUNT(users.user_id) AS user_count, users.country
FROM users
JOIN daily_artist_metrics ON users.country = daily_artist_metrics.country_code
GROUP BY users.country;
items.reason: verification blocked the SQL — Invented join key: 'users.country = daily_artist_metrics.country_code' is not a relationship in the schema — no foreign key links users.country to daily_artist_metrics.country_code, directly or transitively. The legal join chain from users to daily_artist_metrics is: user_follows_artists.user_id = users.user_id ; daily_artist_metrics.artist_id = user_follows_artists.artist_id — join through the intermediate table(s), copying these ON clauses verbatim.

items.nl: Show the name and country of the first five artists from Great Britain, in alphabetical order.
items.reference_sql: SELECT name, country FROM artists WHERE country = 'GB' ORDER BY name LIMIT 5
items.sql:
SELECT name, country
FROM artists
WHERE country = 'Great Britain'
ORDER BY name ASC
LIMIT 5;

items.nl: How many play events does each track have, most played first?
items.reference_sql: SELECT t.title, COUNT(*) FROM tracks t JOIN play_events pe ON t.track_id = pe.track_id GROUP BY t.title ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC

items.nl: How many payments actually went through?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM payments WHERE payment_status = 'completed'

items.nl: How many times has a track actually been played?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM play_events WHERE event_type = 'play'

items.nl: How long is the average track in minutes?
items.reference_sql: SELECT AVG(trk_dur_ms) / 60000.0 FROM tracks
items.sql:
SELECT AVG(trk_dur_ms / 60000.0) AS avg_duration_minutes
FROM tracks;

items.nl: How many users signed up organically?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE usr_acq_src = 1

items.nl: How many standalone singles are in the catalogue?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tracks WHERE album_id IS NULL

items.nl: Which users have ever actually played something?
items.reference_sql: SELECT display_name FROM users WHERE user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM play_events WHERE event_type = 'play')
items.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT u.user_id, u.name
FROM play_events pe
JOIN users u ON pe.user_id = u.user_id
WHERE pe.event_type = 'play';

items.nl: Which tracks has nobody ever liked?
items.reference_sql: SELECT title FROM tracks WHERE track_id NOT IN (SELECT track_id FROM user_liked_tracks)
items.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT t.title, t.trk_dur_ms, t.is_exp, t.isrc, t.track_number, t.total_plays, a.name, a.country, a.bio, a.verified
FROM tracks t
JOIN track_artists ta ON t.track_id = ta.track_id
JOIN user_liked_tracks ult ON ta.track_id = ult.track_id
JOIN artists a ON ta.artist_id = a.artist_id
WHERE t.total_plays = 0
ORDER BY t.title;
caveats: Ambiguous join route: artists and user_liked_tracks were connected through track_artists, but user_follows_artists link them equally directly and answer a different question. The schema alone cannot say which reading you meant.

items.nl: How much revenue did each plan actually bring in?
items.reference_sql: SELECT s.plan_id, SUM(pay.amount) AS revenue FROM payments pay JOIN subscriptions s ON pay.subscription_id = s.subscription_id WHERE pay.payment_status = 'completed' GROUP BY s.plan_id
items.sql:
SELECT ph.plan_id, ph.monthly_price, SUM(pay.amount) AS total_revenue
FROM pricing_history ph
JOIN subscriptions s ON ph.plan_id = s.plan_id
JOIN payments pay ON s.subscription_id = pay.subscription_id
WHERE s.status = 1 AND pay.payment_status = 'completed'
GROUP BY ph.plan_id, ph.monthly_price
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;
caveats: Ambiguous join route: payments and pricing_history were connected through subscriptions, but subscription_periods link them equally directly and answer a different question. The schema alone cannot say which reading you meant.

items.nl: How many plays did each track get in 2026?
items.reference_sql: SELECT t.title, COUNT(*) AS plays FROM tracks t JOIN play_events pe ON t.track_id = pe.track_id WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' AND YEAR(pe.played_at) = 2026 GROUP BY t.title
items.sql:
SELECT t.track_id, t.title, COUNT(*) AS play_count
FROM play_events pe
JOIN tracks t ON pe.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' AND YEAR(pe.played_at) = 2026
GROUP BY t.track_id, t.title
ORDER BY play_count DESC;

items.nl: How many completed payments did each user make during 2025?
items.reference_sql: SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS payments FROM payments WHERE payment_status = 'completed' AND YEAR(payment_date) = 2025 GROUP BY user_id
items.sql:
SELECT 
    u.user_id, 
    COUNT(p.payment_id) AS completed_payments
FROM 
    payments p
JOIN 
    users u ON p.user_id = u.user_id
WHERE 
    p.payment_date >= '2025-01-01' AND p.payment_date <= '2025-12-31' AND p.payment_status = 'completed'
GROUP BY 
    u.user_id;

items.nl: How many distinct listeners does each artist have, by name?
items.reference_sql: SELECT a.name, COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id) AS listeners FROM artists a JOIN track_artists ta ON a.artist_id = ta.artist_id JOIN play_events pe ON ta.track_id = pe.track_id WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' GROUP BY a.name
items.sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT da.artist_id) AS distinct_listeners
FROM artists a
JOIN daily_artist_metrics da ON a.artist_id = da.artist_id
WHERE YEAR(da.metric_date) = 2026 AND da.event_type = 'play'
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY distinct_listeners DESC;
items.reason: verification blocked the SQL — Engine EXPLAIN rejected the query: no such column: da.event_type; Column 'event_type' does not exist in table 'daily_artist_metrics' (referenced as 'da.event_type') — it exists in: play_events

items.nl: Show me all artists from Colombia.
items.reference_sql:
SELECT
    artist_id,
    name,
    country,
    monthly_listeners_cached
FROM artists
WHERE country = 'CO'
items.sql: SELECT artists.name FROM artists WHERE country = 'CO'

items.nl: What is the current monthly price for each subscription plan?
items.reference_sql:
SELECT
    sp.name          AS plan_name,
    ph.monthly_price AS current_price,
    ph.effective_from
FROM subscription_plans sp
JOIN pricing_history ph ON sp.plan_id = ph.plan_id
WHERE ph.effective_to IS NULL
ORDER BY ph.monthly_price
items.sql:
SELECT subscription_plans.name, pricing_history.monthly_price
FROM pricing_history
JOIN subscription_plans ON pricing_history.plan_id = subscription_plans.plan_id
WHERE pricing_history.effective_to IS NULL;

items.nl: Which artists had more than 5 distinct listeners per month in 2024?
items.reference_sql:
SELECT
    a.name                                     AS artist_name,
    DATE_FORMAT(pe.played_at, '%Y-%m')         AS stream_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id)                 AS unique_listeners
FROM artists        a
JOIN track_artists ta ON a.artist_id = ta.artist_id
JOIN play_events   pe ON ta.track_id = pe.track_id
WHERE YEAR(pe.played_at)  = 2024
  AND pe.event_type       = 'play'
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name, stream_month
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id) > 5
ORDER BY stream_month, unique_listeners DESC
items.sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, DATE_FORMAT(dam.metric_date, '%Y-%m') AS stream_month, COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id) AS distinct_listeners
FROM artists a
JOIN track_artists ta ON a.artist_id = ta.artist_id
JOIN play_events pe ON ta.track_id = pe.track_id
JOIN daily_artist_metrics dam ON a.artist_id = dam.artist_id
WHERE YEAR(pe.played_at) = 2024 AND pe.event_type = 'play'
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name, dam.metric_date
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id) > 5
ORDER BY stream_month, distinct_listeners DESC;
caveats: Ambiguous join route: daily_artist_metrics and play_events were connected through track_artists, but user_follows_artists link them equally directly and answer a different question. The schema alone cannot say which reading you meant.

items.nl: Find users who follow at least one artist but have never listened to any music by those specific artists.
items.reference_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
    u.display_name,
    u.country
FROM users u
JOIN user_follows_artists ufa ON u.user_id = ufa.user_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM play_events   pe
    JOIN track_artists ta ON pe.track_id  = ta.track_id
    WHERE pe.user_id   = u.user_id
      AND ta.artist_id = ufa.artist_id
)
items.sql:
SELECT u.user_id, u.name, u.display_name, u.email, u.country, u.birth_date, u.status, u.usr_acq_src, u.joined_at, u.last_login
FROM users u
JOIN user_follows_artists ufa ON u.user_id = ufa.user_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM play_events pe
    JOIN track_artists ta ON pe.track_id = ta.track_id
    WHERE pe.user_id = u.user_id
      AND ta.artist_id = ufa.artist_id
);

items.nl: Show each referred user alongside the display name of who referred them.
items.reference_sql:
SELECT
    u.display_name   AS referred_user,
    u.country        AS referred_country,
    r.display_name   AS referred_by,
    u.joined_at
FROM users u
JOIN users r ON u.referred_by_user_id = r.user_id
ORDER BY u.joined_at
items.sql:
SELECT u.name AS referred_user, u2.display_name AS referred_by
FROM users u
JOIN user_follows_artists uf ON u.user_id = uf.user_id
JOIN users u2 ON uf.followed_by_user_id = u2.user_id
ORDER BY u2.followed_at;
items.reason: verification blocked the SQL — Engine EXPLAIN rejected the query: no such column: uf.followed_by_user_id; Column 'followed_by_user_id' does not exist in table 'user_follows_artists' (referenced as 'uf.followed_by_user_id')

items.nl: How many users do we have?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users
items.sql: SELECT COUNT(user_id) AS total_users FROM users;

items.nl: How many tracks are in the catalogue?
items.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tracks
items.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_tracks
FROM tracks;

items.nl: What's our total revenue to date?
items.reference_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) FROM payments WHERE payment_status = 'completed'
items.sql:
SELECT SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue
FROM payments p
JOIN subscriptions s ON p.subscription_id = s.subscription_id
WHERE s.end_date IS NULL OR s.end_date >= '2026-07-17';

items.nl: What's our average payment size by method?
items.reference_sql: SELECT payment_method, AVG(amount) AS avg_amount FROM payments WHERE payment_status = 'completed' GROUP BY payment_method
items.sql:
SELECT pm.payment_method, AVG(p.amount) AS average_payment_size
FROM payments p
JOIN payment_method pm ON p.payment_method = pm.payment_method_id
GROUP BY pm.payment_method;
items.reason: verification blocked the SQL — Engine EXPLAIN rejected the query: no such table: payment_method; Hallucinated table: 'payment_method' not in DBProfile

items.nl: How do plays break down by device?
items.reference_sql: SELECT device_type, COUNT(*) AS plays FROM play_events GROUP BY device_type
items.sql:
SELECT device_type, COUNT(*) AS play_count
FROM play_events
WHERE event_type = 'play'
GROUP BY device_type;

items.nl: What's the split of our users by acquisition channel?
items.reference_sql: SELECT usr_acq_src, COUNT(*) AS users FROM users GROUP BY usr_acq_src
items.sql:
SELECT 
    u.usr_acq_src,
    u.name,
    u.display_name,
    u.email,
    u.country,
    u.birth_date,
    u.status,
    u.joined_at,
    u.last_login,
    CASE 
        WHEN u.usr_acq_src = 1 THEN 'organic'
        WHEN u.usr_acq_src = 2 THEN 'social'
        WHEN u.usr_acq_src = 3 THEN 'referral'
        WHEN u.usr_acq_src = 4 THEN 'ad'
        ELSE 'unknown'
    END AS acquisition_source_name
FROM 
    users u
JOIN 
    user_liked_tracks ul ON u.user_id = ul.user_id
GROUP BY 
    u.usr_acq_src, u.name, u.display_name, u.email, u.country, u.birth_date, u.status, u.joined_at, u.last_login
ORDER BY 
    u.usr_acq_src, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.usr_acq_src ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC);

items.nl: How many subscriptions sit on each plan?
items.reference_sql: SELECT plan_id, COUNT(*) AS subs FROM subscriptions GROUP BY plan_id
items.sql:
SELECT sp.name, COUNT(*) AS subscription_count
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN subscription_plans sp ON s.plan_id = sp.plan_id
GROUP BY sp.plan_id, sp.name;

items.nl: How many listening hours does each device account for?
items.reference_sql: SELECT device_type, SUM(duration_ms) / 3600000.0 AS hours FROM play_events WHERE event_type = 'play' GROUP BY device_type
items.sql:
SELECT 
    pe.device_type, 
    SUM(pe.duration_ms) AS total_duration_ms
FROM 
    play_events pe
JOIN 
    users u ON pe.user_id = u.user_id
WHERE 
    pe.event_type = 'play'
GROUP BY 
    pe.device_type;

items.nl: What's our revenue by plan?
items.reference_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(pay.amount) AS revenue FROM payments pay JOIN subscriptions s ON pay.subscription_id = s.subscription_id JOIN subscription_plans p ON s.plan_id = p.plan_id WHERE pay.payment_status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.name
items.sql:
SELECT sp.plan_id, SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue
FROM subscriptions sp
JOIN payments p ON sp.subscription_id = p.subscription_id
WHERE sp.status = 1 AND p.payment_status = 'completed'
GROUP BY sp.plan_id;

items.nl: How many distinct listeners does each artist pull in?
items.reference_sql: SELECT ta.artist_id, COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id) AS listeners FROM track_artists ta JOIN play_events pe ON ta.track_id = pe.track_id WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' GROUP BY ta.artist_id
items.sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, SUM(da.stream_count) AS total_listeners
FROM daily_artist_metrics da
JOIN artists a ON da.artist_id = a.artist_id
WHERE YEAR(da.metric_date) = 2026 AND da.country_code = 'CO'
GROUP BY a.name
ORDER BY total_listeners DESC;

items.nl: Who are our top five artists by listener reach?
items.reference_sql: SELECT a.name, COUNT(DISTINCT pe.user_id) AS listeners FROM artists a JOIN track_artists ta ON a.artist_id = ta.artist_id JOIN play_events pe ON ta.track_id = pe.track_id WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' GROUP BY a.name ORDER BY listeners DESC LIMIT 5
items.sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, SUM(dam.stream_count) AS total_stream_count
FROM artists a
JOIN daily_artist_metrics dam ON a.artist_id = dam.artist_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_stream_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

items.nl: Which genres are we biggest in?
items.reference_sql: SELECT g.name, COUNT(*) AS plays FROM genres g JOIN track_genres tg ON g.genre_id = tg.genre_id JOIN play_events pe ON tg.track_id = pe.track_id WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' GROUP BY g.name ORDER BY plays DESC
items.sql:
SELECT g.name, da.stream_count
FROM artist_genres ag
JOIN daily_artist_metrics da ON ag.artist_id = da.artist_id
JOIN genres g ON ag.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE ag.is_prim = 1
ORDER BY g.name, da.stream_count DESC;

items.nl: Who are our most engaged listeners this year?
items.reference_sql: SELECT u.display_name, COUNT(*) AS plays FROM users u JOIN play_events pe ON u.user_id = pe.user_id WHERE pe.event_type = 'play' AND pe.played_at >= '2026-01-01' GROUP BY u.display_name ORDER BY plays DESC LIMIT 10
items.sql:
SELECT u.name, u.display_name, u.email, u.country, u.birth_date, u.status, u.usr_acq_src, u.joined_at, u.last_login, u.referred_by_user_id
FROM users u
JOIN play_events pe ON u.user_id = pe.user_id
WHERE YEAR(pe.played_at) = YEAR(CURDATE())
GROUP BY u.user_id
ORDER BY COUNT(pe.event_id) DESC
LIMIT 10;

items.nl: Show me our best artists.
items.sql:
SELECT a.name, da.stream_count
FROM artists a
JOIN daily_artist_metrics da ON a.artist_id = da.artist_id
ORDER BY da.stream_count DESC
LIMIT 10;
